questions.q: Which relationship describes the marked pair?
questions.tikz: \begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\draw (-2,-1.4)--(2,1.4);\draw (-2,1.4)--(2,-1.4);\draw (0.42,0.3) arc[start angle=35,end angle=145,radius=0.52];\draw (-0.42,-0.3) arc[start angle=215,end angle=325,radius=0.52];\end{tikzpicture}

options.why: Chooses angles across the vertex even though they share no arm.
Infers a $90$ degree total without any angle measures.
Infers a $180$ degree total without any angle measures.
Uses a total of $180$ degrees, identifying a supplementary angle.
Assumes equal measures automatically form a complementary pair.
Subtracts from $100$ instead of from $90$ degrees.
Uses a total of $90$ degrees instead of $180$ degrees.
Subtracts from $190$ instead of from $180$ degrees.
Assumes equal measures automatically form a supplementary pair.

options.text: They share a vertex and one arm without overlapping.
They lie across from each other at an intersection.
options.why: Uses the positional definition of vertically opposite angles.
Uses the measure definition of complementary angles.
Uses the measure definition of supplementary angles.
